elements.name: Description
texts: "It is in the neighbourhood of [the islands beyond Station Island] that the fish are caught, and round them the boat made its slow circuit, as the fishermen, keeping accurate time, cast, with lines not above twice the length of their rods, the one to the right and the other to the left of its course."

elements.name: Source
texts: Newland, Henry, The Erne, Its Legends and Its Fly-Fishing, pp. 220-21

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Internet Archive, originally from University of California

elements.name: Text
texts: "It is in the neighbourhood of [the islands beyond Station Island]  that the fish are caught, and round them the boat made its slow circuit, as the fishermen, keeping accurate time, cast, with lines not above twice the length of their rods, the one to the right and the other to the left of its course."
